title: Clippy: 11 #[must_use] candidates — add attribute to return-value-only methods
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension:** Documentation & Intent, Functional Correctness
**Lint:** clippy::must_use_candidate

## Violation
Pure functions and accessor methods that return a non-`()` value without side effects should be marked `#[must_use]` so the compiler warns when callers discard the result. Otherwise silent bugs (forgetting to assign the builder chain result, ignoring an `Option`-like query).

## Locations
Functions (6):
- src/modes/fragments.rs: 57, 66, 75, 84
- src/modes/mod.rs: 427, 465

Methods (5):
- src/modes/mod.rs: 290, 363, 378, 397, 409

## Mandated Refactor
```rust
#[must_use]
pub fn as_system_fragment(&self) -> String { ... }
```
All flagged sites are in `src/modes/` — consider a crate-level `#![warn(clippy::must_use_candidate)]` in that module to enforce going forward.
